items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

children.content: WA Police say a 16-year-old boy is in custody suspected of stabbing his mother to death and seriously injuring his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

gallery.altText: Two police forensic officers and a detective in a suit stand behind police tape near two police vehicles on a suburban street.
Three police officers stand behind police tape on the front verge of a house in a suburban street.
A police officer kneels down next to a police vehicle at a crime scene marked by police tape at an intersection.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused young men at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a light grey flat cap and black coat leavs court.

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest

children.content: A handcuffed teenager was injured when he was forced to the ground by a police officer, sparking a WA Police review after video of the incident emerged.

items.title: 'Don't go there': Hanson fires up in Perth over White Australia policy

children.content: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

image.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

items.title: How authorities failed to heed one woman's reports of WA's worst child rapist

children.content: Maggie Dawkins's efforts to shine a light on the horrific abuses committed by Dennis McKenna against a generation of boys in Katanning are set down in a new book.

image.altText: Dennis McKenna in brown shirt, tie and jacket on the left. On the right a photo of brick buildings.

items.title: UK YouTuber Yung Filly violently sexually assaulted young fan, court hears

children.content: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.

gallery.altText: Yung Filly shot from the side as he walks into court.
Yung Filly smiling on stage holding a microphone.
Yung Filly in a gray suit posing in front of a car.

items.title: Synagogue had requests for security funding rejected before firebombing

children.content: Jewish leaders have given evidence to the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion regarding security concerns for synagogues nationwide.

image.altText: Firefights walk past fire damage and smashed glass at the Synagogue.

items.title: PMOS/PCOS linked to 'significantly higher' stroke, heart attack risk

children.content: Women with polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS, formerly PCOS) have a "significantly higher" risk of cardiovascular disease than those without, a new study suggests.

image.altText: Practitioner is examining patient checking blood pressure and pulsethen talking to young woman during medical visit.

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Annabel Crabb and Anthony Albanese stand smiling on the porch of an old building

items.title: Woman critically injured after stabbing in Kalgoorlie

children.content: Part of the mining city's main street was blocked off and detectives are investigating after the woman was found with serious stab wounds.

image.altText: A police forensics officer at a crime scene.

items.title: Five-year wait for much-needed housing in WA's holiday hotspot

children.content: A housing charity says bureaucratic delays have held up its attempt to build some small homes in Margaret River.

gallery.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Two men point at an old railway barracks

thumbnail.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Concept art of the small homes proposed for Margaret River by MyHome.
Two men point at an old railway barracks

items.title: Kids do feel the cold. So why won't they wear a jumper?

children.content: Do kids really not feel the cold like adults do? Or are they just expressing their independence? And when should you insist?

items.title: US Army veteran arrested after FBI building 'attacked' during ICE protest

children.content: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

image.altText: A fire producing dark black smoke rises from a NY footpath

items.title: What's behind the protests in New Delhi?

children.content: Thousands of protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party have marched on India's parliament and clashed with the authorities. Here's why.

image.altText: A protester wearing a cockroach mask waves a fist.

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Decade of work helps remote island's native wildlife 'return to 1616'

children.content: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.

gallery.altText: A small kangaroo-like marsupial jumps on sand.
A small rat-like animal wrapped in a purple blanket.

A small rat-like animal wrapped in a purple blanket.

items.title: Ian doesn't want children. He's part of a growing trend among men

children.content: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

image.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

items.title: Australian EV owners deal with 'range anxiety'. China has almost eliminated it

children.content: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

image.altText: A young boy poking his head into the open passenger door of a vehicle with yellow seat covers

items.title: The four sets of data that show how the property market is transforming

children.content: Global instability, shifting tax policies and interest rate pressures force buyers and sellers to adjust to the changing property landscape.

image.altText: Houses under construction on a bright, sunny day

items.title: How this Champions League winner aims to ignite Stingers

children.content: Winning the Champions League with Greek giants Olympiacos was "one of the coolest moments" of Abby Andrews's life. She wants to use that experience to push the Stingers to the next level.

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

children.content: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

image.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

items.title: 'Sit down!' Joyce's dog interrupts 7.30 interview as he defends Hanson

children.content: The One Nation MP says Pauline Hanson's meeting with far-right British activist Tommy Robinson did not amount to an endorsement of his criminal conduct, rejecting suggestions she had crossed a "red line".
The otherwise serious 7.30 interview was twice interrupted by Mr Joyce's dog, prompting him to yell at it to "sit down".
